rulings.Case Title: IN RE: STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of This Court'S January 31, 2022 Order Terminating Jurisdiction Of Petitioner'S Law Practice; Motion For Accounting Of Property Seized Pursuant To Order For Assumption Of Law Practice
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2025, Line 1. RESPONDENT DANIEL EVERETT STATE BAR MEMBERSHIP NO. 268967 Motion For Reconsideration Of This Court'S January 31, 2022 Order Terminating Jurisdiction Of Petitioner'S Law Practice; Motion For Accounting Of Property Seized Pursuant To Order For Assumption Of Law Practice. Off calendar. Judge Schulman is available to hear reconsideration of his prior order. Please contact the clerk in courtroom 304 to obtain a new hearing date and time. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: CREDITORS ADJUSTMENT BUREAU, INC. VS. SENIOR HEALTHCARE ADVISORS LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Five9, Inc.S Compliance With Deposition And Document Subpoena; Request For Contempt And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2025, Line 10. DEFENDANT SENIOR HEALTHCARE ADVISORS LLC AKA SENIOR HEALTHCARE ADVISORS' Motion To Compel Five9, Inc.S Compliance With Deposition And Document Subpoena; Request For Contempt And Monetary Sanctions. Defendant Senior Healthcare Advisors' motion to compel third party Five9 to appear for deposition is granted and its request for monetary sanctions is denied. Five9 must appear for deposition on a mutually agreeable date no later than March 7, 2025 and produce all non-privileged documents requested. In the event Five9 contends that any requested documents are privileged, Five9 must idenity all such documents in a privilege log. Senior Healthcare has shown good cause for the deposition and document production. Monetary sanctions are denied because Five9 did not lack substantial justification in failing to attend a deposition just prior to an ex parte hearing on a request to continue the trial where Senior Healthcare did not communicate its intent to go forward with the deposition notwithstanding the close proximity in time to the ex parte hearing and the parties dispute whether there was an understanding whether deposition dates were to be continued due to the ex parte application. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ANGIE MILON VS. JOHN MUNGUIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Second Motion For Order Compelling George G. Benetatos To Serve Supplemental Responses To Written Discovery, Monetary Sanction; Memorandum
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2025, Line 11. DEFENDANTs JOHN MUNGUIA,and TRAD'R SAM's Motion For Order Compelling George G. Benetatos To Serve Supplemental Responses To Written Discovery, Monetary Sanction. Cross-complainants Trad'r Sam and John Munguia's motion for order compelling cross-defendant George Benatos to serve supplemental discovery responses is granted and Mr. Benatos is required to pay $3,000 in reasonable monetary sanctions to Trad'r Sam and Mr. Munguia. Mr. Benatos' continued adherence to his objections based on Civil 1714.10 and refusal to provide any substantive responses to the discovery covered by this motion after the issuance of the July 10, 2024 order contravened the explicit language of that order, are without merit and lack substantial justification. Mr. Benatos must provide supplemental discovery responses and pay the monetary sanctions no later than March 7, 2025. rulings.Case Title: REV CAPITAL (CALIFORNIA) INC. VS. LEGAL FREIGHTLINES LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2025, Line 5. PLAINTIFF REV CAPITAL (CALIFORNIA) INC. APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Plaintiff Rev Capital's application for right to attach order and writ of attachment is denied. There are disputed issues precluding issuance of a right to attach order and writ of attachment regarding the existence and amount of unpaid invoices and late charges. Specifically, the court is unable to determine from the evidence submitted by the parties that there is probably validity to Rev Capital's claim for $120,825.70 in unpaid invoices and $386,038.91 in late charges. Notably the amount of the late charges is more than three times the amount of the unpaid invoices raising a host of concerns not addressed by Rev Capital including but not limited to whether the late charges are unconscionable fees and/or invalid liquid penalties. Moreover, Rev Capital has not shown that its claim is unsecured or partially secured. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: GENOA MARTELL VS. CONTEXTLOGIC, INC. (DBA "WISH)", A CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2025, Line 6. DEFENDANT WISH US HOLDINGS LLC, QOO10PTE LTD DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT. Defendants Wish US Holdings LLC and Q00010PTE LTD's demurrer to the first amended complaint is off calendar and plaintiff Genoa Martell is granted leave to file a second amended complaint no later than March 7, 2025. The court construes Ms. Martell's opposition papers as requesting an opportunity to file an amended complaint to allege additional facts and possibly new claims. In keeping with California's strong public policy favoring liberal granting of amendments to pleadings, Ms. Martell is granted leave to file a second amended complaint. If Ms. Martell does not file a second amended complaint by March 7, 2025, demurring defendants may re-notice their demurrer to the first amended complaint. If Ms. Martell chooses to file a second amended complaint by March 7, 2025, in doing so she should keep in mind that a complaint must allege ultimate facts, not mere conclusions, supporting each claim alleged against each defendant. If demurring defendants choose to file a demurrer to the second amended complaint, in an effort to avoid repetition of the accusations that each side made against the other regarding their meet and confer session as to this demurrer, the meet and confer session regarding the demurrer to the second amended complaint should occur by a remote platform such as Zoom and each side and the entirety of the session should be recorded. In the event that there is any accusation of misconduct by either side, the court will likely want to view the recording. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: **Transferred to TULARE County** FERNANDO ANDRADE ET AL VS. AMERICAN HONDA MOTOR CO., INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2025, Line 13. PLAINTIFFs FERNANDO ANDRADE,and SULEMA LOZANO's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiffs' Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Plaintiffs' motion to compel further responses to their set one production of documents is granted. Defendant American Honda must produce all non-privileged responsive documents and serve further responses confirming that it has done so no later than April 30, 2025. If American Honda withholds any documents on the grounds of privilege, the withheld documents must be identified in a privilege log. Based on the supplemental declaration of Roy Enav, the court deems this motion filed as of November 5, 2024 and thus it is timely filed. Plaintiffs have shown that they seek production of discovery relevant documents and American Honda has failed to show that production of those documents imposes an undue burden on it. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: JESUS TORRES VS. DOORDASH INC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion Of Motion To File A Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Calendar for Thursday, February 13, 2025, Line 8. PLAINTIFF JESUS TORRES' Motion To File A Second Amended Complaint. Plaintiff Jose Torres' motion to file a second amended complaint is granted. Although Mr. Torres did not file a proof of service, no defendant has appeared in the case and he has not yet filed any proof of service of a complaint or summons on any defendant. Consequently, as far as any defendant is concerned, the second amended complaint is likely the first complaint that it will see and will suffer no prejudice from the granting of permission to Mr. Torees to file the second amended complaint. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. (302/HEK)
